source_display: Power User Paradox (Technical vs Systems Thinking)
evidence: Vrijen described himself as becoming a '1% or 0.1% power user' of products he embeds with—someone who dives so deep into a platform that he reaches functionality most users never discover. Yet he simultaneously identifies as 'non-technical but increasingly trying to push my boundaries'
Vrijen has moved 12 cities across 33 years. 'Perhaps frequent relocation doesn't just make you adaptable—it makes you good at noticing adaptation.'

Zain: 'I started gravitating towards this theme of, like, a lot of people are talking about AI, but what does it mean for Human side... And so how do we make sure or how do we ensure that as we have more automation in workplace, as we see more AI and adoption, how do we ensure that's actually good for us as humans'
Vrijen: 'that's highly congruent' - validated this framing as aligned with his own thinking on human-centered AI

source_display: Small World Algorithm as Trust Mechanism
evidence: The Logan connection moment: 'We've built digital systems designed to filter down to the most relevant connections, but the moments that actually create trust are often the unfiltered overlaps that slip through'

Vrijen: 'Desktop optimization is ultimate like you can rationalize it as being highly relevant because it's your day to day space but it is also such a like complete like luxury. Like a like it's an indulgence'
Vrijen: 'I have long been of the belief that, like, if you have substantive things to say, you don't need to rely on these like, cheap tactics'
Zain: 'One of my beliefs too is like, I mean for most part it's like collaboration over competition'
Small world moment: 'we got connected through Zo' - Zain had independently connected with Logan on TikTok before meeting Vrijen
Vrijen: 'check out fohi. I think obviously it's literally right up your alley. I'm actually about to be announced as a co lead for nyc'
Careerspan isn't a replacement for Handshake—it's the 'missing layer' that makes their products defensible. Distribution platforms solved access, but access without signal is noise.

Handshake has 1,600 universities but no way to capture behavioral signal at scale. Careerspan has coaching infrastructure to capture signal but lacks distribution. The combination creates a data moat neither could build alone.

Extended strategy discussion positioning Careerspan as strategic hedge for Handshake's declining core business ($200M, only 6% growth). New domain: positioning as 'band-aid hedge' for specific acquirer.

The entire Careerspan thesis rests on premise that behavioral signal (how people think) is more valuable than profile data (what they've done). This challenges fundamental premise of LinkedIn and traditional resumes.

Emphasis on month-on-month retention and return rates reveals long-term data play. The real value isn't in helping someone get one job—it's in tracking their entire career trajectory and predicting future success.

Emphasizing the 3-person team as asset (low acquisition overhead) turns potential weakness into strength. In era of bloated startups with high burn rates, being lean makes you attractive acquisition target.
Both Vrijen and Logan seeing surprising social media growth (20% and 2,000 followers respectively) with minimal posting. Educational content about careers and recruiting has organic demand.
Logan also experiencing organic growth on content - validates pattern across both founders
Careerspan's positioning around 'elite talent identification' represents strategic pivot from general career coaching. The real money in recruiting isn't in volume—it's in high-value placements.
Extended conversation about Handshake partnership opportunity. Handshake has 1,600 universities, $200M revenue but only 6% growth. Looking for foothold in next business direction (elite talent).

source_display: Decision: Scale Outbound via SDR Hiring
evidence: V: 'We need to scale the outbound.' N agreed and proposed hiring more SDRs. V committed: 'I'll talk to the board.'
